campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council is holding public hearings on renewing an interim ordinance tied to a state Senate law and on revoking business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for two local shops, California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop. The council will also vote on applying for a grant to fund public works projects and on approving new consultant service agreements.
keyTopics: Renewal of interim ordinance implementing a state Senate bill
Revocation of business license and tobacco retailer permit for California Cigar
Revocation of business license and tobacco retailer permit for Hayes Smoke Shop
Public Works Director authorized to submit a grant application
Approval of new consultant service agreements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council is moving forward with an annual assessment district process, taking preliminary steps toward approving a special report and setting up a public hearing before a related ordinance comes up for final adoption later in May. The Council will also look at whether Saratoga should consider becoming a charter city, and will review early estimates of possible new tax options and how much revenue they might bring in.
keyTopics: Preliminary approval of the Engineer's Report for the assessment district
Resolution of Intention and public hearing on the assessment
Ordinance scheduled for adoption at the May 20 meeting
Discussion of charter city status and next steps
Review of potential new tax options and revenue estimates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council's April 21, 2026 meeting covers routine business like approving prior meeting minutes and reviewing February's financial report, alongside approving a construction contract and a three-year professional services agreement. The council will also consider an amendment to an existing agreement and hear court-ordered appeals, with time set aside for public comment on select agenda items.
keyTopics: Construction contract award to QLM, Inc.
Resolution directing preparation of town improvement plans
Three-year professional services agreement approval
Second amendment to an existing town agreement
Court-ordered hearing on appeals

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting covered routine approvals of past council and committee meeting minutes, along with a few business items. The council reviewed a change to a retail concession contract at the airport, an update to airport fees charged to airlines and other users, and approved a trip for the City Manager to travel to Tampa, Florida.
keyTopics: Approval of prior City Council meeting minutes
Approval of Council Committee meeting minutes
Amendment to the retail merchandising concession agreement with PRI-San José, LLC at the airport
Update to airport fees and charges rate structure
City Manager's approved travel to Tampa, Florida

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to discuss city finances, water planning, and several public works projects, alongside a few honorary proclamations. Council also met privately with legal counsel about potential lawsuits facing the city, and celebrated City Attorney Jennifer Logue's appointment to the judiciary.
keyTopics: Closed-session discussion on two potential lawsuits against the city
Proclamations for National Immigrant Heritage Month, the Kiwanis Club's 100th anniversary, and City Attorney Jennifer Logue's judicial appointment
Setting the city's spending limit and updating budget policy for fiscal year 2026-27
Adoption of the 5-year Urban Water Management Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan
Capital projects including the Public Safety Building, Shoreline Golf Links parking lot repaving, and South Bay Salt Pond Restoration

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — July 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: Sunnyvale's City Council held a special study session and covered routine business, including a discussion on banning chemical pesticides on city-owned or leased property. Council also approved standard items like meeting minutes, bill payments, a library materials contract, and a nearly $1.4 million construction contract to fix a levee at the water treatment plant.
keyTopics: Proposal to eliminate chemical pesticide use on city-owned or leased property
5:30 PM special study session preceding the regular meeting
$1,391,426 contract awarded to Brannon Corporation for levee repairs at the Water Pollution Control Plant's oxidation pond
Extension of the library's agreement with Brodart Co. for books and shelf-ready materials
Routine approvals: city bill payments and June 16 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met in a closed session with its legal counsel to discuss three ongoing lawsuits against the city. These discussions covered strategy for pending court cases and were not open to public debate, as is standard for active litigation matters.
keyTopics: Lixin (Leon) Chen, et al. v. City of Cupertino lawsuit
Yes in My Backyard, et al. v. City of Cupertino lawsuit
Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety v. City of Cupertino lawsuit
Closed-session legal strategy discussion with city attorneys

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 23, 2026
santa-clara.summary: This notice mainly covers logistics for the Santa Clara City Council meeting rather than specific agenda items. Residents can attend in person or join remotely via Zoom or phone, and can submit written comments in advance through the city's online eComment system.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote participation)
Zoom and phone dial-in access details for the meeting
How to submit written public comment before the meeting via eComment

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council will review a proposed change to how federal housing and community development funds are allocated under the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan. Councilmembers will also discuss new project ideas they want the city to work on and consider changing the rules for how a vacant council seat gets filled, including shortening timelines involved in that process.
keyTopics: Proposed amendment to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 funding plan (affects federal housing/community development dollars)
New project and policy ideas submitted by individual councilmembers for future city work
Proposed changes to the ordinance governing how a vacant council seat is filled, including reducing certain timeframes
Upcoming City Council meeting schedule and calendar
Preview of items expected at the next regular Council meeting

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council honored retiring city official Bill Gutoff with a formal proclamation of appreciation. The bulk of the meeting focused on a required public hearing (Proposition 218) on proposed increases to water, wastewater, and refuse (garbage) utility rates, with the Finance Committee recommending Council adopt several resolutions setting new rate schedules and financial plans for these utilities.
keyTopics: Retirement proclamation honoring Bill Gutoff
Proposition 218 public hearing on utility rate increases
Water utility rate changes (Schedules W-1 through W-4 and W-7) and financial forecast
Wastewater collection rate changes (Schedules S-1, S-2, S-6, S-7) and reserve management
Refuse/garbage collection rate changes (Schedules R-1 and R-C)
